channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: As elite Davos event ends, some hail 'constructive optimism' despite divisions, suffering in world
item.description: The World Economic Forum's annual gathering of elites in Davos has ended with many business leaders, world-class academics, top government officials and other elites casting an upbeat tone about economic prospects, efforts to end wars and technological progress.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten And David Mchugh, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: UK warns of Iranian attempts to force ships into its waters
item.description: A warning has gone out to seafarers in the Persian Gulf over what appear to be attempts by Iran’s Revolutionary Guard to compel ships to enter Iranian waters. While Iran has a history of harassing and impounding vessels, it hasn’t directly launched many such attacks recently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netanyahu suggests Israel might not complete its withdrawal from Lebanon by a ceasefire deadline
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has suggested that Israel might not withdraw all of its forces from Lebanon by a deadline set in its ceasefire with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippines launches comic book to counter China’s 'disinformation' in the disputed South China Sea
item.description: The Philippines has launched a comic book to counter what the country says is China’s disinformation campaign to push its expansive territorial claims in the South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN suspends all trips into Houthi-held areas of Yemen over staffers being detained
item.description: The United Nations on Friday suspended all travel into areas held by Yemen’s Houthi rebels after more of their staff were detained by the rebels.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea prepares to send more troops to Russia after suffering casualties, South Korea says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s military said Friday it suspects North Korea is preparing to send additional troops to Russia after its soldiers fighting in the Russian-Ukraine war suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major storm lashes Ireland and Scotland as hurricane-force winds down power lines and ground flights
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Millions of people in Ireland and northern parts of the U.K. were urged to stay at home Friday as hurricane-force winds disabled power networks and brought widespread travel disruptions.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After 80 years, not many Auschwitz survivors are left. One man makes telling the stories his mission
item.description: HAIFA, Israel (AP) — Naftali Fürst will never forget his first view of the Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp, on Nov. 3, 1944. He was 12 years old. SS soldiers threw open the doors of the cattle car, where he was crammed in with his mother, father, brother, and more than 80 others.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis await names of next 4 hostages to be released by Hamas as part of the ceasefire
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Relatives of hostages still being held by militants in Gaza called on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu Friday to ensure all remaining captives are freed, while also appealing to U.S. President Donald Trump to continue pressing for their release.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban reject court move to arrest top officials for persecuting Afghan women and girls
item.description: The Taliban Friday rejected a court move to arrest two of their top officials for persecuting women, accusing the court of baseless accusations and misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine is reforming its recruitment efforts to attract younger soldiers and boost forces
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine is in the final stages of drafting recruitment reforms to attract 18- to 25-year-olds who are currently exempt from mobilization as it looks for ways to bolster its fighting force, the battlefield commander recently appointed to the President’s Office said.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump urges Putin to ‘make a deal’ to end the war in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump on Thursday urged Russian President Vladimir Putin to make a deal to end the war in Ukraine, arguing he thinks the latter is ready to negotiate.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Davos 2025: Trump leaves an impression with his video address to the World Economic Forum
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump drew pockets of laughter and a few moans with his blunt comments to an international audience while appearing by video link Thursday at the World Economic Forum meeting in Davos, Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A 2,000-year-old statue is found abandoned in a garbage bag in Greece
item.description: A marble statue believed to be more than 2,000 years old has been found in a garbage bag near the city of Thessaloniki in Greece; experts say the piece dates back to the Hellenistic era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Thai LGBTQ+ couples register marriages as law gives them equal status
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Hundreds of LGBTQ+ couples in Thailand made Thursday a life-changing occasion, registering their marriages legally on the first day a law took effect granting them the same rights as heterosexual couples.
item.dc:creator: CHALIDA EKVITTHAYAVECHNUKUL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump redesignates Houthis as foreign terrorist organization
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday directed his administration to relist the Houthis in Yemen as a foreign terrorist organization (FTO) in an effort to crack down on the Iranian-backed militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: 66 dead after fire erupts at popular ski hotel in Turkey
item.description: At least 66 people died and 51 others became injured in a fire that broke out at a Turkish ski resort hotel on Tuesday. Over 200 guests were staying at the hotel when the disaster struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli intel indicates Hamas held hostages at new Gaza hospital as UN health agency criticized for inaction
item.description: The IDF recently completed a major raid at the Kamal Adwan Hospital in the northern Gaza Strip, arresting some 240 terrorists. Fox News Digital has learned that hostages were reportedly held there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners as part of cease-fire deal to free hostages
item.description: Israel released 90 Palestinian prisoners to cheering crowds in the West Bank after Hamas returned three hostages that were being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel, Hamas cease-fire deal could enable rearming of Gaza terrorists
item.description: As the cease-fire deal is underway between Israel and Hamas terrorists, some military and political commentators in Israel are worried that the deal will enable the Iran-backed terror group to rearm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
